citizenRegistration.title: Citizen Registers a New Digital Address
citizenRegistration.context: A citizen named María González has recently moved to a newly developed neighborhood in Malabo. She needs to register her new residence to obtain an official digital address for banking, utility services, and legal documentation.
citizenRegistration.objective: Submit a new residential address request through the unified workflow
citizenRegistration.presenterNotes: The unified flow eliminates confusion - citizens don't need to understand NAR vs CAR vs Business. The system guides them through the appropriate process based on their selection.

1.action: María accesses the system at the home page
1.screen: Index page with hero section and "Iniciar Sesión" button
1.notes: Point out the clean, professional government design. Mention multi-language support (EN/ES/FR).

2.action: She clicks "Iniciar Sesión" and logs in with her credentials
2.notes: Highlight secure authentication. Mention first-time users can register here.

3.action: After login, system detects her "citizen" role and displays her dashboard
3.notes: Point out role-based navigation - citizens see only relevant options.

4.action: María clicks "Register Address" in the sidebar
4.notes: Explain the unified flow handles residential AND business registrations.

5.action: She searches for her address by entering street name "Avenida de la Libertad 45"
5.screen: AddressLookupStep with search input and results
5.notes: Show how the system searches existing NAR addresses first.

6.action: No existing address is found. María clicks "Create New Address"
6.notes: Point out the system guides users - it won't create duplicates.

7.notes: Highlight the clear distinction between residential and business.

8.action: María uses the interactive map to pinpoint her exact location
8.notes: Demonstrate clicking on map to set GPS coordinates. Mention offline fallback.

9.action: She fills in: Street, City, Region, Building number, Photo of property
9.screen: Form fields populate, camera capture opens
9.notes: Show the camera permission request. Emphasize geotagged photos.

10.notes: Note that citizens provide justification for audit trail.

11.notes: Show the instant confirmation. Explain request goes to verification queue.

12.action: María navigates to "Request Status" to track her submission
12.notes: Demonstrate transparency - citizens can always check status.

outcomes: María has submitted her address request in under 5 minutes
She has a reference number for tracking
The request enters the NAR verification queue
Upon approval, she'll receive a unique UAC (Unified Address Code)

municipalityValidation.title: Municipality Validates and Manages an Address
municipalityValidation.context: Carlos Mendez, a NAR Verifier assigned to Bioko Norte region, reviews María's address request and validates it for inclusion in the National Address Registry.
municipalityValidation.objective: Review and approve a pending address request with proper verification
municipalityValidation.presenterNotes: The verification workflow ensures data quality. Geographic scope filtering means each verifier manages only their territory. The auto-publishing policy eliminates manual steps for commercial addresses while protecting residential privacy.

1.action: Carlos logs in with his verifier credentials

2.action: His dashboard shows role badge "Verifier" with scope "Bioko Norte"
2.screen: Dashboard header with role badges and geographic scope indicator
2.notes: Highlight geographic scope filtering - he only sees requests for his region.

3.action: Stats cards show: 3 NAR Addresses, 5 Pending Verifications
3.screen: Overview stats cards with real-time counts
3.notes: Point out pending count is filtered by his assigned region.

5.action: He sees María's request at the top of the pending list
5.notes: Note requests are ordered by submission date.

6.action: Carlos clicks "View Details" on María's request
6.notes: Point out comprehensive data available for verification.

7.action: He clicks "View on Map" to verify coordinates
7.screen: AddressMapDialog opens showing location on interactive map
7.notes: Demonstrate satellite view toggle for visual verification.

8.screen: System runs coordinate validation, shows recommendations

9.action: He reviews the geotagged photo showing the property facade
9.screen: Photo viewer with metadata (timestamp, GPS coordinates)
9.notes: Highlight photo evidence chain for legal compliance.

10.screen: Confirmation dialog with checkbox for verification notes

11.action: He adds note and confirms approval
11.notes: Explain audit trail of who approved and when.

12.notes: Point out the unique address code format.

13.action: For residential addresses, the address is set to private by default
13.notes: Explain auto-publishing policy: Commercial = public, Residential = private.

outcomes: María's address is now officially in the National Registry
She receives a UAC code: GQ-BN-MLO-AVLB-0045
Audit trail shows Carlos verified it with timestamp
Address is searchable by authorized systems

businessVerification.title: Business Validates an Address for Service Delivery
businessVerification.context: BANKIA Financial Services needs to verify a customer's address before opening a bank account. The compliance officer searches the public address registry to confirm the address exists.
businessVerification.objective: Verify an address through the public portal without authentication
businessVerification.presenterNotes: The public portal enables instant address verification for businesses, reducing onboarding time from days to minutes. The QR code provides portable, scannable proof of address verification.

1.action: Ana accesses the public portal without logging in
1.notes: Emphasize: No login required for public searches.

2.action: She sees two tabs: "Address Search" and "Business Directory"
2.screen: Tabs component at top of portal

3.notes: Show how UAC provides instant precise lookup.

5.screen: Search result card with verified badge, completeness score
5.notes: Point out the "Verified" badge indicating official status.

6.action: Ana clicks "View Details" on the result
6.screen: Expanded card showing full address details
6.notes: Note: Coordinates are approximate for privacy (residential).

7.action: The card shows verification status: ✓ Verified, Score: 95%
7.notes: Explain completeness score = data quality indicator.

8.action: Ana clicks "Generate QR Code" for documentation
8.screen: QRCodeGenerator dialog opens with QR preview
8.notes: Show QR contains the UAC for easy mobile scanning.

9.action: She downloads the QR code as proof of verification
9.notes: Explain this creates auditable proof of verification.

10.action: For business addresses, Ana checks the "Business Directory" tab
10.notes: Show integrated business directory with commercial addresses.

11.action: She searches for "BANKIA" to find bank branch locations
11.screen: Search results showing bank branch addresses with details

outcomes: Ana verified the customer's address in under 2 minutes
She has QR-coded proof of verification for compliance records
The bank can proceed with account opening
No manual verification or phone calls required

carRegistrationHousehold.title: CAR Registration, Household Management & Address Verification
carRegistrationHousehold.context: Pedro Martínez has his NAR address approved and now needs to declare it as his official residence (CAR). He also wants to register his household members including his spouse and two children, and request residency verification for legal purposes.
carRegistrationHousehold.objective: Declare a CAR address, create a household group with members and dependents, and request residency verification
carRegistrationHousehold.presenterNotes: CAR registration connects citizens to their addresses for government services. Household management enables family grouping with proper roles (head, member, dependent). The verification request system provides official proof of residence for legal, banking, and administrative purposes. Note how privacy controls give citizens power over their data visibility.

1.action: Pedro logs into his citizen dashboard
1.screen: UnifiedDashboard with citizen navigation showing My CAR Addresses, Household, Verification Requests
1.notes: Point out the CAR-specific menu items available to citizens.

2.action: He clicks "My CAR Addresses" in the sidebar
2.screen: CitizenAddressManager showing current addresses (if any) and "Add Address" button
2.notes: Explain CAR = Citizen Address Registry - links citizens to their official residence.

3.action: Pedro clicks "Add Address" and enters his approved UAC: GQ-BN-MLO-AVLB-0045
3.screen: Address lookup by UAC with verified address result
3.notes: Show that CAR declaration uses existing NAR addresses - no duplicates created.

4.notes: Explain: Citizens can have one primary and multiple secondary addresses.

5.action: Pedro sets privacy level: "Private - Authorized Services Only"
5.notes: Highlight citizen control over who can see their address.

6.action: He confirms the declaration and address is added
6.screen: Success message, address appears in "My Addresses" list with "Self-Declared" status
6.notes: Note: Self-declared status means awaiting verification.

7.notes: Explain households group family members at same address.

8.screen: CreateHouseholdDialog with household name, primary UAC fields

9.action: Pedro enters: Household Name: "Martínez Family", Primary Address: his CAR address
9.screen: Form fields populated, Create button enabled
9.notes: Point out automatic linking to CAR address.

10.action: Household created. Pedro is automatically set as "Household Head"
10.screen: Household card with Pedro as head, "Add Member" and "Add Dependent" buttons
10.notes: Explain the household head role and permissions.

11.action: He clicks "Add Member" to add his spouse Carmen
11.screen: AddMemberDialog with person search or email invite
11.notes: Members must have their own accounts to be added.

12.action: Pedro enters Carmen's email for invitation
12.screen: Email invitation form with relationship selector: Spouse
12.notes: Show relationship types: Spouse, Parent, Sibling, etc.

13.action: Invitation sent. He now clicks "Add Dependent" for children
13.notes: Dependents are minors or adults without their own accounts.

14.action: Pedro adds first child: Name, DOB, Relationship: Son
14.screen: Dependent form with full_name, date_of_birth, relationship_to_guardian
14.notes: Explain dependent types: Minor, Adult Dependent, Elderly Dependent.

15.action: He adds dependent type: "Minor" and saves
15.screen: Success message, dependent appears in household member list
15.notes: Point out guardian relationship is recorded.

16.screen: Household now shows 2 dependents, 1 pending member (Carmen)

17.action: He navigates to "Verification Requests" to request residency verification
17.notes: Explain residency verification = official proof of residence.

18.action: Pedro clicks "Request Verification" for his primary address
18.screen: Verification request form with address selector, purpose field

19.action: He selects purpose: "Legal Documentation" and submits
19.notes: Explain the request goes to CAR verification queue.

20.screen: Request card with status badge, timeline
20.notes: Demonstrate tracking capability for verification requests.

outcomes: Pedro has declared his official residence (CAR) linked to verified NAR address
Household "Martínez Family" created with 4 members: Pedro (head), Carmen (pending), 2 children (dependents)
Carmen will receive invitation to join household when she logs in
Residency verification request submitted for legal purposes
All family members can be located via household address for government services

passportDeliveryCar.title: Government Postal Delivery to Citizen Residence
passportDeliveryCar.context: The Immigration Office needs to deliver a newly issued passport to citizen Elena Nguema at her registered CAR address. The postal delivery agent uses the ConEG Postal Module to verify the address, navigate to the location, and confirm delivery with proof.
passportDeliveryCar.objective: Use the Postal Delivery Module with CAR address data to deliver official government documents
passportDeliveryCar.presenterNotes: This scenario demonstrates the real-world value of CAR addresses for government service delivery. The UAC provides a universal identifier that ensures documents reach the correct person at the correct location. Digital signatures and GPS verification create an audit trail that protects both the citizen and the government agency. This workflow eliminates failed deliveries and reduces administrative overhead.

1.action: Immigration Office creates delivery order with Elena's UAC: GQ-BN-MLO-ELEN-0089
1.screen: Delivery order creation interface with UAC field
1.notes: Show how government agencies use UAC for citizen identification.

2.action: System automatically retrieves CAR address details for the UAC
2.screen: Address details populated: Street, City, Region, GPS coordinates
2.notes: Explain automatic address lookup from CAR registry.

3.action: Delivery agent Roberto logs in with his government credentials
3.screen: Authentication page, redirect to delivery dashboard
3.notes: Point out role-based access for delivery personnel.

4.action: Roberto sees today's delivery assignments in his queue
4.screen: Delivery queue with pending items, Elena's passport at top

5.action: He clicks on Elena's delivery to view full details
5.screen: Delivery detail card: recipient name, UAC, full address, special instructions

6.screen: Interactive map showing exact location with satellite imagery
6.notes: Show GPS precision for accurate delivery.

8.action: Roberto drives to the location following GPS directions
8.notes: Note: Real-time tracking available for supervisors.

9.action: Upon arrival, he clicks "Arrived at Location"
9.notes: System verifies agent is within proximity of address.

10.action: Roberto verifies the address matches physical location
10.screen: Address confirmation screen with UAC and street details
10.notes: Show visual confirmation step for delivery accuracy.

11.action: He rings doorbell and Elena answers

12.action: Roberto asks Elena to verify her identity (National ID shown)
12.notes: Point out security measures for sensitive documents.

13.action: Elena confirms receipt by signing on the device screen
13.screen: Signature capture pad with "Sign here" prompt
13.notes: Show digital signature capture for proof of delivery.

14.action: Roberto takes a photo of the delivery (optional)
14.notes: Explain photo evidence for delivery confirmation.

16.action: System records: delivery time, location, recipient signature

17.action: Elena receives SMS/email notification: "Your passport has been delivered"

18.screen: Back-office view with delivery status updated, timestamp, signature
18.notes: Show end-to-end tracking visibility for government.

outcomes: Passport delivered accurately to Elena's CAR-registered address
Complete audit trail: who delivered, when, where, with proof
Citizen received notification of successful delivery
Immigration Office has digital proof of delivery with signature
No failed deliveries due to incorrect address information

emergencyCall.title: Emergency Call Linked to a Digital Address
emergencyCall.context: Juan Pérez witnesses a traffic accident near Avenida de la Independencia in Malabo. He uses the emergency alert feature to report the incident with his precise location.
emergencyCall.objective: Report an emergency with GPS coordinates linked to the address system
emergencyCall.presenterNotes: The emergency alert system captures precise GPS coordinates and automatically routes to the appropriate dispatcher based on location. The system auto-assigns to an available dispatcher in the geographic area. Citizens receive real-time updates on response progress.

1.action: Juan opens the app (already logged in as citizen)

2.action: He clicks "Emergency Contacts" in the navigation
2.screen: EmergencyContacts component with Police and Emergency cards

3.screen: Emergency cards with phone call and location alert buttons
3.notes: Note both direct call AND digital alert options.

4.action: Juan clicks "Send Location Alert" on Police card
4.notes: Explain this triggers GPS location capture.

5.action: System requests location permission and captures coordinates

6.notes: Point out the high accuracy for emergency response.

7.action: Juan types message: "Traffic accident, two vehicles, possible injuries"

8.screen: Confirmation dialog with warning about emergency use
8.notes: Show the confirmation step to prevent accidental alerts.

9.notes: Note: Juan receives reference number for tracking.

10.action: System automatically looks up nearest NAR address
10.screen: Background process - links to UAC if nearby

11.action: Juan sees notifications panel with status updates
11.notes: Demonstrate real-time status tracking for reporters.

outcomes: Emergency alert created with precise GPS coordinates
Incident auto-assigned to available dispatcher in Malabo
Reporter has reference number and can track response
System linked alert to nearest verified address (UAC)

dispatcherAssignment.title: Dispatcher Assigns Emergency Units
dispatcherAssignment.context: Dispatcher Elena García receives Juan's emergency alert at the police command center. She must assign responding units and coordinate the response.
dispatcherAssignment.objective: Assign and coordinate field units to respond to an emergency incident
dispatcherAssignment.presenterNotes: The threaded communications system with sound notifications ensures dispatchers never miss critical alerts. Radio code quick buttons speed up standard communications. The three-tab structure (Inbox/Sent/History) keeps communications organized.

1.action: Elena is logged in at police command center

2.screen: Toast notification appears with incident summary
2.notes: Demonstrate audio alerts - different tones for priority levels.

3.action: New incident appears in incident list with "PENDING" status
3.screen: IncidentList with new card highlighted in amber

4.screen: Incident card with type, priority, location summary
4.notes: Explain auto-classification based on emergency type.

5.action: Elena clicks the incident to view full details

6.action: She reviews the Map tab showing exact incident location
6.screen: IncidentMap with incident marker and nearby units
6.notes: Point out unit positions on map for proximity assignment.

7.screen: Unit overlay on map with status badges
7.notes: Explain real-time unit status from GPS tracking.

8.screen: Assignment dialog with available units checkbox list

9.action: Elena selects "Alpha-7" and adds dispatch note
9.notes: Note dispatcher can add context for field units.

10.screen: System sends assignment, updates incident status

12.action: She sends quick message using radio code "10-23 En Route"
12.screen: Quick radio code buttons below compose area
12.notes: Show how radio codes speed up communication.

13.action: Message appears in Sent tab with timestamp
13.screen: Sent messages list with delivery confirmation
13.notes: Point out the message threading by unit.

outcomes: Incident assigned within 45 seconds of alert receipt
Field unit Alpha-7 receives notification with location details
Dispatcher can track unit progress on map
All communications logged for audit trail

fieldResponder.title: Field Responders Use Address Data on the Ground
fieldResponder.context: Officer Miguel Rodríguez of Unit Alpha-7 receives the dispatch assignment on his mobile device. He navigates to the scene, manages the incident, and captures evidence.
fieldResponder.objective: Navigate to incident, manage response, and capture evidence with GPS data
fieldResponder.presenterNotes: Field officers have everything they need on mobile: navigation to exact coordinates, evidence capture with geolocation, status updates, and emergency backup requests. The "Officer Down" button provides critical safety functionality with immediate system-wide broadcast.

1.action: Miguel opens the app on his mobile device

2.action: His UnitFieldDashboard shows current assignment notification
2.screen: Assignment card with incident details, Accept button

3.notes: Show the status workflow: Accept → En Route → On Scene.

4.action: Miguel clicks "Navigate" button to get directions

5.screen: Distance estimate from current GPS to incident

6.action: Native maps app opens with route to incident coordinates

7.action: While en route, Miguel updates status: clicks "En Route"

8.notes: Point out response time tracking starts now.

9.action: He uses "Capture Evidence" button to photograph the scene
9.screen: Camera interface opens with GPS overlay

10.action: Miguel takes photos of: accident scene, vehicle damage

11.action: Photos upload to secure storage with GPS coordinates
11.notes: Note: Evidence stored in Supabase storage bucket.

12.action: Miguel needs additional resources, clicks "Request Resources"
12.notes: Show the detailed resource request options.

13.action: He selects: Type: Medical, Urgency: High, Quantity: 1 ambulance
13.screen: Form fields for resource type, urgency, quantity

14.action: Resource request sent, visible to dispatchers

15.action: Critical situation: Miguel presses "OFFICER DOWN" button
15.notes: CRITICAL: Explain this broadcasts to ALL police staff immediately.

outcomes: Officer navigated directly to scene using digital address coordinates
Evidence captured with GPS metadata for legal chain of custody
Response time automatically tracked: Dispatch → Arrival = 3 minutes
All actions logged for supervisor review and audit

supervisorMonitoring.title: Supervisor Monitors and Reports
supervisorMonitoring.context: Captain Isabel Ruiz, Police Supervisor, reviews the day's operations including the traffic accident response. She monitors unit performance and generates reports for command staff.
supervisorMonitoring.objective: Monitor operations, approve backup requests, and generate performance reports
supervisorMonitoring.presenterNotes: Supervisors have full oversight with approval authority for backup requests. Real-time analytics enable data-driven decisions. The audit log ensures complete transparency - every action is tracked with who, what, and when.

1.action: Captain Ruiz logs in and sees supervisor dashboard

2.action: Overview shows: 12 Active Incidents, 8 Available Units, Avg Response: 4.2 min
2.screen: Stats cards with real-time operational metrics
2.notes: Point out real-time data from database.

3.action: She clicks "Units Overview" to see all unit status
3.screen: UnitsOverview component with unit cards and status

5.action: She sees a pending backup request from Unit Bravo-3
5.notes: Point out tiered approval: Supervisors approve, Dispatchers coordinate.

6.action: Captain approves the backup request with note
6.notes: Show approval workflow and notification to requesting unit.

8.action: She selects date range: "Last 7 Days" using calendar picker

9.action: Charts show: Response times, Incidents by type, Unit utilization
9.notes: Point out the visual analytics dashboard.

10.action: Captain drills into the traffic accident incident

11.action: She reviews the Evidence tab showing captured photos
11.screen: Evidence gallery with photos, timestamps, locations

13.action: Logs show all actions: dispatch, status updates, evidence uploads
13.screen: Log entries with actor, action, timestamp

14.action: She exports the day's report using "Export CSV" button
14.notes: Show reporting capability for command staff.

outcomes: Supervisor has real-time visibility into all operations
Backup requests handled with proper authority hierarchy
Performance metrics available for resource planning
Complete audit trail ensures accountability
Exportable reports for command briefings

postalDeliveryWorkflow.title: Complete Postal Delivery Module Workflow
postalDeliveryWorkflow.context: The ConEG Postal Delivery Module manages government postal services from order creation to final delivery. This enhanced scenario demonstrates recipient search by name, in-app routing, Cash on Delivery (COD), pickup requests, returns, bulk import, and complete delivery workflow.
postalDeliveryWorkflow.objective: Demonstrate enhanced postal features: recipient name search, in-app routing, COD, pickups, returns, and bulk import
postalDeliveryWorkflow.presenterNotes: This enhanced postal scenario showcases all Phase 1-8 features: recipient name search (including household dependents), in-app routing with Leaflet/OSRM (eliminates need for external navigation apps), COD handling from collection to remittance, pickup request service for citizen convenience, returns/reverse logistics for failed deliveries, bulk import for high-volume operations, and automated customer notifications. The integration with the digital address system ensures accurate delivery using UAC-verified addresses.

1.action: Carmen (Postal Clerk) logs into the Postal Portal
1.screen: PostalPage with Clerk view showing "Create Order" and "Bulk Import" tabs
1.notes: Point out the role-based interface with enhanced features for postal staff.

2.action: She clicks "Create New Order" to begin order entry
2.notes: Explain the multi-step order creation process.

3.action: Carmen enters sender info: Government Immigration Office
3.screen: Sender section with branch, name, phone fields
3.notes: Show sender details including branch identification.

4.action: Carmen searches by recipient NAME: "Pablo Martínez Jr." (a household dependent)
4.screen: UACAddressPicker shows name search results including dependents
4.notes: NEW: Recipient search by name finds household members/dependents, not just account holders.

5.action: System shows Pablo's address with "Care of: Pedro Martínez" (guardian)
5.screen: Address card with dependent badge and guardian relationship
5.notes: Demonstrate household dependent address resolution for postal delivery.

6.action: Carmen adds package details: Passport, Registered, Requires ID verification
6.screen: Package type selector, special handling checkboxes
6.notes: Show package classification and security options.

7.action: She enables Cash on Delivery (COD) and enters amount: 5,000 XAF
7.screen: CODSection with amount field and payment method options
7.notes: NEW: COD allows payment collection at delivery.

8.action: Carmen generates shipping label with barcode and QR code
8.screen: LabelPreview showing S10 tracking number, barcode, QR code
8.notes: NEW: UPU S10-compliant labels with scannable codes.

9.action: Order submitted with number: DEL-2025-001234 and tracking: EG123456789GQ
9.screen: Success confirmation with order and S10 tracking numbers
9.notes: Note: Order enters dispatcher queue; tracking URL in QR code.

10.action: Luis (Dispatcher) views orders in "Ready for Assignment" tab
10.screen: DispatcherPanel with pending orders list including COD indicators
10.notes: Dispatchers see orders waiting for agent assignment with COD flags.

11.action: He opens "Pickups" tab to see citizen pickup requests
11.screen: PickupRequestsList showing pending pickup requests from citizens
11.notes: NEW: Citizens can request package collection from their addresses.

12.action: Luis schedules a pickup request and assigns to Pedro
12.screen: Pickup scheduling dialog with date/time and agent selection

13.action: He assigns both the delivery and pickup to Pedro García
13.screen: Assignment dialog with route optimization suggestion
13.notes: Demonstrate workload balancing and route efficiency.

14.action: Pedro (Agent) receives notifications on his mobile device
14.screen: Agent mobile view with delivery and pickup assignments
14.notes: Point out mobile-first design for field agents.

15.action: Pedro clicks "Show Route" for embedded in-app navigation
15.screen: RouteMapView with visual route polyline, markers, distance, ETA, turn-by-turn directions
15.notes: NEW: In-app routing using Leaflet/OSRM - no external app needed.

16.action: Route shows: Origin (blue marker) → Destination (red marker), 2.3km, 8 min
16.screen: Interactive map with auto-fitted bounds and scrollable directions
16.notes: Demonstrate embedded navigation keeping agent in app.

17.action: Pedro arrives and clicks "Mark Arrived" - system logs GPS
17.notes: Show location verification for delivery accuracy.

18.action: He collects COD payment: 5,000 XAF cash and records receipt
18.screen: COD collection dialog with amount, payment method, receipt number
18.notes: NEW: COD collection tracked with receipt for remittance.

19.action: Pedro captures proof: photo, signature, recipient name
19.screen: Proof capture: photo, signature pad, recipient name confirmation
19.notes: Demonstrate multi-method proof capture for accountability.

20.action: Delivery completed - Elena receives SMS/email notification
20.screen: Customer notification: "Your package has been delivered"
20.notes: NEW: Automated customer notifications at key lifecycle stages.

21.action: Pedro completes the pickup, captures photo proof

22.screen: CODManagement showing pending collections and daily reconciliation
22.notes: NEW: COD tracking from collection to remittance.

23.action: She views agent COD balances and marks Pedro's 5,000 XAF as remitted
23.screen: Agent remittance dialog with amount and reference

24.action: Ana opens "Returns" tab to manage reverse logistics
24.screen: ReturnOrdersList showing return requests and their status
24.notes: NEW: Returns management for undeliverable or customer-initiated returns.

25.action: Carmen uses "Bulk Import" to upload 50 orders from CSV
25.screen: BulkImportDialog with CSV upload, validation preview, batch processing
25.notes: NEW: Bulk order import with validation and error reporting.

outcomes: Recipient search by name enables delivery to household dependents without UAC
In-app routing keeps agents in the app with visual navigation and directions
COD collection tracked from payment to supervisor remittance
Pickup requests allow citizens to schedule package collection
Returns/reverse logistics manage undeliverable and customer-returned packages
Bulk import processes high-volume orders efficiently via CSV upload
Automated customer notifications at all delivery lifecycle stages
S10-compliant labels with barcode and QR codes for tracking
Complete audit trail with GPS, photos, signatures, and COD receipts
